codeReview.summary: The rerun fix commit did not acceptably resolve the UI-proof issues. It stabilized the targeted tests by introducing a simulated-success path and by weakening the home-selection assertion to accept blocker states, so the tests could pass without proving the required visible post-onboarding and active-home behavior.

issues.description: When `--simulate-home-ready` is active, the `.noHomeConfigured` blocker path calls `completeOnboarding()` instead of remaining blocked. That converts a failed Home-readiness result into onboarding success, so `OnboardingFlowTests.testCompletedOnboardingDoesNotReappearOnRelaunch` can pass without reaching a legitimate visible setup/home-selection state.

`testHomeSelection_ShowsHomeDetails` was loosened to pass if blocker screens such as `Set Up Apple Home First` or `Home Access Needed` appear. That no longer proves visible home-selection details or active-home choice as required by the feature; it treats failure/loading states as success.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The swiftui-feature-worker procedure says reset hooks must never auto-complete blocked flows into success semantics, but this worker introduced exactly that shortcut for `--simulate-home-ready`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `DawnLoopApp/Onboarding/HomeAccessFlowView.swift` in commit `c6ab2e2f79397180585a538ef3115971dfcbd430` completes onboarding from the `.noHomeConfigured` branch when `TestEnvironment.isSimulatingHomeReady` is true; the worker handoff also states it updated test assertions to handle blocker states.

summary: Reviewed the fix feature handoff, prior failed review, transcript skeleton, skill guidance, and the actual fix commit `c6ab2e2f79397180585a538ef3115971dfcbd430`. Result: fail, because the fix achieved green targeted UI tests by adding a simulated-success backdoor and by accepting blocker states in the strict home-selection test, so it did not genuinely resolve the UI-proof concerns.
